title: Smoky Silky German Bread Soup
description: A cozy, plant‑based German soup that blends the heartiness of rye bread with a silky tofu‑oat milk base, finished with smoky paprika and fresh herbs.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180 °C (350 °F). Toss the bread cubes with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp smoked paprika, and a pinch of smoked salt. Spread them on a baking sheet and roast for 10 minutes until golden and slightly crisp. Set aside.
In a large pot, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion and minced garlic; sauté until translucent, about 4–5 minutes.
Stir in the smoked paprika, smoked salt, and ground cumin, cooking for 1 minute to release the spices’ aromas.
Pour in the vegetable broth and oat milk, bring to a gentle simmer, then add the roasted bread cubes. Let simmer for 8 minutes, allowing the bread to soften and the broth to thicken.
While the soup simmers, blend the silken tofu with a splash of oat milk until completely smooth and silky. Add this mixture to the pot, whisking well to combine.
Taste and adjust seasoning; add lemon juice if needed. Reduce heat to low and let the soup thicken further for 3–4 minutes.
Remove from heat, stir in chopped parsley, and ladle the soup into bowls. Garnish with a few extra bread cubes if desired.
Serve hot, paired with a side of fresh greens or steamed vegetables.
